Re: Request for CoDfirnation of Telephone Advice
Dear Jeanne:
This letter requests written confirrnation of our recent
telephone conversation regardingT the sender identification
requirernents of a slate mailer organization. In our
conversation, I indicated that a client of this firn has been
asked to make a pa)rnent to a slate mail-er organization to support
several candidates and a measure on a slate mailer being produced
by the slate mailer organization. The slate mail,er organj-zation
has been in existance for a number of years and done mailings in
a nunber of prior el-ections. I indicated that the payment by our
client would not be rrat the behesttt of any of the candidates nor
of the balIot neasure comrnittee and therefore wouLd, i.n our
opinion, constitute an independent expenditure to support those
candidates and the measure.
I also indicated that the payment being requested from our
cfient may be more than 502 of aII palments received for the
slate mail-er. Our client is advised by the slate mai1er
organization that other candj-dates and measures wil-I be rnaking
payments to the sl-ate mailer organization to appear on the slate
rnailer and the sfate rnailer organization may also put some
candidates and neasures on the slate without having received a
paynent .
f advised you that we understand that the slate mailer
organization complies with covernment Code Section 824Oa.4; that
it wil-l produce one or rnore slate mailers, as that terrn is
defined i-n Government Code Section a2O4A.3; that is has
registered with the California Secretary of state pursuant to
Governnent Code Section 841,08; that it intends to discfose its
activities pursuant to Government Code Section 84218 and A42L9;
and that if it receives contributions immediately prior to the
efection it will file reports pursuant to covernment code Section
84220.


Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
May 87 19 91
Page 2
Based on the above facts we asked the following questions
and you provided the fol,Iowing answers:
1. 
QueEtioD: What rrsender identificationrr is required on
the slate mailer produced by the slate
organi z at ion?
The slate rnailer organj-zation must meet the
identifj,cation and disclai-mer requirements of
covernment Code Section 84305.5.
Specifically, the slate mail,er must include
the name, street address and city of the
slate rnailer organization on the outside of
each piece of slate mail and on at l-east one
of the inserts in no l-ess than 8 point roman
type which sha11 be in a col-or or print which
contrasts with the background so as to be
easily 1egib1e. At the top or botton of the
front side or surface of at l-east one insert
or at the top or bottom of one side or
surface of a post card or other self maj-1er
there is a notice of at least 8 point roman
bold face type notice which shall- be in a
color or print which contrasts with the
background so as to be easily legible and
dra!,/n in a box. The notice sha1l consist of
the folIoe/ing statenent:
l{ot ice to voters
This docurEnt ras paepared by (NarE of State l,{aiter Organization), not an
official. potitical, party o.ganization- Appea.ance in this lrEiter does not
necessari [y ioply erdorsement of others in this IIEiler. Appearances paid
for and authorized by each cardidate ard battot flEasure rhich is designated
Answef 3
2. 
Question: If our clientrs palrment constitutes the
najority of all payments received for the
productj.on of the slate mailer, is the mass
nail,ing identification 
nurnber described in
Governnent Code Section 84305 required?
No. The reguirement for the slate mailer
organization takes precedent.
Answer:


Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
May 8, 1991
Page 3
3. 
QuestioD:
Can the rrNotice to Votersrr be nodified to add
a sentence to identify payments received by
the slate mailer organization which
constitute 'r independent expendituresrt, as
that term is defined in covernnent Code
Section 82031 , to support candidates or
support or oppose ballot neasures?
The sl,ate nailer statutes do not contemplate
a pa)rment to a slate mailer organization
which is an independent expenditure and
instead only contemplate direct pa)rnents by
candidate or baIIot neasure cornrnittees or
payments by third parties at the behest of
candidates or ballot measure co:nmittees. You
indicated that this additionaf Ianguage to
the Notice to voters, while not reguired by
the statute, does provj,de further and nore
complete disclosure of the transactions.
Such additional disclosure r.rould be
considered in furtherance of the Act.
Answer:
I believe that this letter accurately reflects our
telephone conversation. Please provide written confirmation of
this telephone advice at your earliest convenience. If vou have
any quesr:ions or need any additionaL infornation, please do not
hesitate to contact me.
